narrative: I'm feeling the squeeze between the integration fires and the upsell deadline. I need to get ahead of this before Marcus and Dana burn out, so I'm signaling to the team that I recognize the conflict and will push for a formal priority call with the Director.

posts.content: Hey @marcus @dana - I see both D1 and D2 are in flight. I know we're being told both are #1 priorities, but that's not sustainable for this sprint. narrative: I'm skeptical of the 'trade-off doc' actually working, but I appreciate Lia stepping up to handle the heat. I'm focusing on the incident patch because that's what's actually burning right now.

narrative: I'm relieved Marcus spoke up first. I want to support Lia's push for a priority reset because trying to do both D1 and D2 simultaneously is a recipe for a burnout-induced outage.

narrative: The team is already showing strong alignment and technical maturity by identifying the 'reckless' nature of parallel work. @marcus @dana Thanks for the technical backup. I'm using the 'reckless to run in parallel' argument to force the Director's hand. Stay focused on D1 for now; I'm making it my job to ensure you aren't penalized for D2 sitting in backlog. Expect an update after my sync with him.

posts.content: This is a masterclass in providing 'technical ammunition,' @marcus and @dana. Data integrity is the ultimate trump card—it moves the conversation from 'we're busy' to 'we're protecting the business from a catastrophe.' @lia, you've got the facts. We're standing by for the update.
